mission.title: Install and Configure Trivy Operator (Aqua Security)
mission.description: Trivy Operator is a Kubernetes-native security toolkit that helps you identify vulnerabilities in your container images and deployed applications. It is useful for ensuring compliance and security in your Kubernetes clusters. Key trade-offs include the need for proper RBAC configurations and potential performance overhead during vulnerability scanning.

steps.title: Add the Aqua Security Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Aqua Security Helm repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Trivy Operator.

```bash

helm repo add aqua https://aquasecurity.github.io/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Aqua Security Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Trivy Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Trivy Operator using Helm, specifying the namespace and version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace trivy-system

helm install trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

```

> Creates a dedicated namespace for the Trivy Operator.
> Installs the Trivy Operator in the trivy-system namespace.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Trivy Operator pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Lists all pods in the trivy-system namespace to verify the installation.

steps.title: Create a TrivyConfiguration resource
steps.description:
This step creates a TrivyConfiguration custom resource to define how the Trivy Operator should scan images.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: trivy-config
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  image: docker.io/aquasec/trivy:0.21.0
  severity: HIGH, CRITICAL
EOF

```

> Creates a configuration for scanning images with specified severity levels.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: trivy-config
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  image: docker.io/aquasec/trivy:0.21.0
  severity: HIGH, CRITICAL
EOF

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Trivy Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Trivy Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that any configurations and scan results will be lost.

```bash

helm uninstall trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system

kubectl delete namespace trivy-system

```

> Uninstalls the Trivy Operator from the trivy-system namespace.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Trivy Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important configurations. This step upgrades the Trivy Operator to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get trivyconfiguration -n trivy-system -o yaml > trivy-config-backup.yaml

helm upgrade trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Backs up the current TrivyConfiguration.
> Upgrades the Trivy Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get trivyconfiguration -n trivy-system -o yaml > trivy-config-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'trivy-operator not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Trivy Operator has not been installed in the specified namespace.

**Fix:**
Ensure that you have installed the Trivy Operator in the correct namespace using the Helm install command.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Trivy Operator may be misconfigured or unable to pull the required images.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n trivy-system' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: No vulnerabilities found when expected
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Trivy Operator may not be scanning the correct namespaces or images.

**Fix:**
Verify the TrivyConfiguration resource and ensure it targets the correct images and namespaces.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'permission denied'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient RBAC permissions for the Trivy Operator.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the necessary RBAC roles and bindings are correctly configured for the Trivy Operator.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for custom image repositories and improved scanning performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Trivy Operator pod running in the 'trivy-system' namespace with a status of 'Running'. Use 'kubectl get pods -n trivy-system' to verify.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.25 or higher and the necessary tools installed.
